Docs
Deployment Guide

Deployment Guide

# Overview

JephyPHP Framework Documentation can be deployed across various environments, from budget shared hosting to enterprise cloud platforms. This guide covers configuration, server setup, and optimization for each platform.

Important: Before deployment, ensure config.conf has debug = false and appropriate caching is enabled.

Pre-Deployment Checklist

Before deploying to any environment:

# 1. Set debug mode to false
# Edit jephy-mvc/config.conf
[app]
site.debug = false                    # Important for production

# 2. Enable Smarty caching
[smarty]
smarty.caching = true
smarty.cache_lifetime = 3600           # 1 hour default

# 3. Set proper file permissions
chmod -R 755 jephy-mvc/app/cache/
chmod -R 755 public/assets/
chmod 644 jephy-mvc/config.conf

# 4. Remove development tools and test files
rm -rf tests/ (if not needed)

# 5. Secure your .htaccess (if using Apache)

1. Shared Hosting Deployment

Shared hosting is the most restrictive environment but Jephy-MVC works well with proper configuration.

Requirements for Shared Hosting

  • PHP 8.0+ (check with your host)
  • Apache with mod_rewrite (most common)
  • MySQL/MariaDB database
  • FTP/SFTP or cPanel File Manager access

Step-by-Step Deployment

Step 1: Prepare your project locally

# Create a deployment package (excluding development files)
cd your-project
zip -r jephy-mvc-deploy.zip . -x "*.git*" "*.github*" "tests/*" "docs/*"
Step 2: Upload to shared hosting

Using FTP/cPanel:
1. Upload the ZIP file to your hosting root (usually public_html or www)
2. Extract the ZIP file
3. Your structure should look like:
   public_html/
   ├── composer.json
   ├── composer.lock
   ├── jephy-mvc/
   ├── public/
   └── vendor/
Step 3: Configure document root

Most shared hosts point to public_html/ as root. You need to point it to public/:

Option A: Using .htaccess (in public_html)

# Place this in your root public_html/.htaccess
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !^/public/
RewriteRule ^(.*)$ /public/$1 [L]
Option B: If host allows custom document root
  • Set document root to public_html/public/
  • Contact support if needed
Step 4: Configure database

# 1. Create database via cPanel/phpMyAdmin
# 2. Update jephy-mvc/config.conf

[database]
host = "localhost"          # Or your host's MySQL hostname
name = "your_cpanel_dbname"
user = "your_cpanel_user"
password = "your_password"
driver = "mysql"
Step 6: Test deployment
Visit: https://yourdomain.com

Shared Hosting Troubleshooting

Issue Solution
404 errors on routes Check mod_rewrite is enabled. Ensure .htaccess is in public/ folder
White screen Check jephy-mvc/app/cache/ is writable (chmod 755)
Smarty compile errors Verify template_dir and compile_dir paths in config.conf
Database connection failed Host may require non-standard port or socket. Check with hosting provider

VPS / Dedicated Server Deployment

VPS gives you full control. Recommended for production applications.

Recommended Stack
  • Web Server: Nginx (preferred) or Apache
  • PHP: 8.0+ with FPM
  • Database: MySQL 8.0 / PostgreSQL 14+
  • OS: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS / Debian 12

Nginx + PHP-FPM Setup (Recommended)

Step 1: Install required packages

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update
sudo apt install nginx php8.1-fpm php8.1-mysql php8.1-pgsql php8.1-curl php8.1-mbstring

# Install Composer if needed for vendor packages
curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php
sudo mv composer.phar /usr/local/bin/composer
Step 2: Upload your project

# Clone from GitHub
cd /var/www
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/jephy-mvc.git myapp
cd myapp

# Or upload via SCP
scp -r ./jephy-mvc root@your-server:/var/www/myapp/
scp -r ./public root@your-server:/var/www/myapp/
scp -r ./vendor root@your-server:/var/www/myapp/
Step 3: Set permissions

cd /var/www/myapp
sudo chown -R www-data:www-data jephy-mvc/app/cache/
sudo chmod -R 755 jephy-mvc/app/cache/
sudo chmod -R 755 public/assets/
Step 4: Configure Nginx site

Create /etc/nginx/sites-available/jephy-mvc:


server {
    listen 80;
    server_name yourdomain.com;
    root /var/www/myapp/public;
    index index.php;

    # Logs
    access_log /var/log/nginx/jephy-mvc-access.log;
    error_log /var/log/nginx/jephy-mvc-error.log;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$args;
    }

    location ~ \.php$ {
        include snippets/fastcgi-php.conf;
        fastcgi_pass unix:/var/run/php/php8.1-fpm.sock;
        fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
        include fastcgi_params;
    }

    location ~ /\.ht {
        deny all;
    }

    # Assets caching
    location ~* \.(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|ico|css|js)$ {
        expires 1y;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }
}
Step 5: Enable site and restart

sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/jephy-mvc /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl restart nginx php8.1-fpm
Step 6: Configure SSL with Let's Encrypt

sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx
sudo certbot --nginx -d yourdomain.com

Apache Setup (Alternative)

Step 1: Enable mod_rewrite

sudo a2enmod rewrite
sudo systemctl restart apache2
Step 2: Configure VirtualHost

<VirtualHost *:80>
    ServerName yourdomain.com
    DocumentRoot /var/www/myapp/public
    
    <Directory /var/www/myapp/public>
        Options -Indexes +FollowSymLinks
        AllowOverride All
        Require all granted
    </Directory>
    
    ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/jephy-mvc-error.log
    CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/jephy-mvc-access.log combined
</VirtualHost>

3. Cloud Platform Deployment

Amazon Web Services (AWS)

Option A: AWS Elastic Beanstalk (Easiest)
Step 1: Install EB CLI
pip install awsebcli --upgrade
Step 2: Prepare your application
# Create .ebignore file (similar to .gitignore)
echo "vendor/" > .ebignore

# Create .ebextensions/php.ini for custom settings
mkdir .ebextensions
.ebextensions/php.ini:

files:
  "/etc/php.d/99-jephy-mvc.ini":
    mode: "644"
    owner: root
    group: root
    content: |
      upload_max_filesize = 64M
      post_max_size = 64M
      memory_limit = 256M
Step 3: Deploy to Elastic Beanstalk

eb init -p "PHP 8.1" jephy-mvc-app
eb create jephy-mvc-prod --envvars APP_ENV=production
eb deploy
Option B: AWS EC2 (Full control)
Step 1: Launch EC2 instance
  • Amazon Linux 2023 or Ubuntu 22.04
  • t3.micro (free tier) or larger
  • Security group: Allow HTTP (80), HTTPS (443), SSH (22)
Step 2: Connect and setup

ssh -i your-key.pem ec2-user@your-ec2-public-dns

# Install dependencies (Amazon Linux)
sudo yum update -y
sudo yum install -y httpd php8.1 php8.1-mysqlnd git

# Clone your project
sudo git clone https://github.com/yourusername/jephy-mvc.git /var/www/html/
cd /var/www/html

# Set permissions
sudo chown -R apache:apache jephy-mvc/app/cache/
Step 3: Configure RDS database

# Create RDS MySQL instance via AWS Console
# Update jephy-mvc/config.conf with RDS endpoint

# jephy-mvc/config.conf
# [database]

driver = "mysql" # or pgsql for Postgres SQL
host = "your-db.xxxxxx.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com"
name = "jephy_db"
user = "admin"
password = "your_password"
Step 4: Set up S3 for file storage (optional)

// Use AWS SDK for PHP to store user uploads in S3
composer require aws/aws-sdk-php
Option C: AWS Lightsail (Simpler VPS)
  • Choose LAMP stack (PHP 8.x)
  • Deploy via SSH or Lightsail deployment tool
  • Built-in firewall and snapshots

Google Cloud Platform (GCP)

Option A: Google App Engine (Serverless)
Step 1: Install Google Cloud SDK

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash
gcloud init
Step 2: Create app.yaml

runtime: php81
env: standard

runtime_config:
  document_root: public

env_variables:
  APP_DEBUG: false
  APP_ENV: production

automatic_scaling:
  min_instances: 1
  max_instances: 10

handlers:
- url: /assets
  static_dir: public/assets
  expiration: "30d"

- url: /.*
  script: auto
Step 3: Deploy

gcloud app deploy
gcloud app browse
Option B: Google Compute Engine (VPS)
Step 1: Create VM instance

gcloud compute instances create jephy-mvc-vm \
    --zone=us-central1-a \
    --machine-type=e2-micro \
    --image-family=ubuntu-2204-lts \
    --tags=http-server,https-server
Step 2: SSH and setup

gcloud compute ssh jephy-mvc-vm

# Install LAMP stack
sudo apt update
sudo apt install apache2 php8.1 libapache2-mod-php8.1 php8.1-mysql git

# Deploy your app
sudo git clone https://github.com/yourusername/jephy-mvc.git /var/www/html/
Step 3: Configure Cloud SQL
  • Create Cloud SQL MySQL instance
  • Update config.conf with Cloud SQL connection details
  • Use Cloud SQL Proxy for secure connection
Option C: Google Cloud Run (Container)

dockerfile

# Dockerfile
FROM php:8.1-apache
COPY . /var/www/html/
RUN chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html/jephy-mvc/app/cache/
EXPOSE 80



bash

# Build and deploy
gcloud builds submit --tag gcr.io/your-project/jephy-mvc
gcloud run deploy jephy-mvc --image gcr.io/your-project/jephy-mvc 
--platform managed

Microsoft Azure

Option A: Azure App Service (Recommended)
Step 1: Install Azure CLI

bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash
az login
Step 2: Create App Service
# Create resource group
az group create --name jephy-mvc-rg --location eastus

# Create App Service plan
az appservice plan create --name jephy-mvc-plan --resource-group 
jephy-mvc-rg --sku B1 --is-linux

# Create web app with PHP 8.1
az webapp create --resource-group jephy-mvc-rg --plan jephy-mvc-plan 
--name jephy-mvc-app --runtime "PHP|8.1"
Step 3: Deploy from GitHub

bash

az webapp deployment source config --name jephy-mvc-app --resource-group 
jephy-mvc-rg --repo-url https://github.com/yourusername/jephy-mvc 
--branch main --manual-integration
Step 4: Configure environment variables

bash

az webapp config appsettings set --name jephy-mvc-app --resource-group 
jephy-mvc-rg --settings APP_DEBUG=false DB_HOST=...
Step 5: Configure Azure Database for MySQL

bash

# Create MySQL server
az mysql server create --name jephy-mysql --resource-group jephy-mvc-rg 
--location eastus --admin-user jephyadmin --admin-password YourPassword 
--sku-name B_Gen5_1

# Update config.conf with Azure MySQL endpoint
Option B: Azure Virtual Machines

bash

# Create VM
az vm create \
    --resource-group jephy-mvc-rg \
    --name jephy-vm \
    --image UbuntuLTS \
    --admin-username azureuser \
    --generate-ssh-keys

# SSH and setup LAMP (similar to VPS section)
az vm open-port --port 80 --resource-group jephy-mvc-rg --name jephy-vm
Option C: Azure Container Instances

bash

# Quick container deployment
az container create \
    --resource-group jephy-mvc-rg \
    --name jephy-mvc-container \
    --image yourdockerhub/jephy-mvc:latest \
    --dns-name-label jephy-mvc-app \
    --ports 80

Environment-Specific Configuration

Production config.conf Template

#	config.conf


[app]
name = "My Jephy-MVC App"
debug = false
timezone = "UTC"

[paths]
site.home  = "https://yourdomain.com"
site.url.home  = "https://yourdomain.com/assets/"

[database]
host = "localhost"              # Update for cloud DB
name = "jephy_prod"
user = "prod_user"
password = "strong_password"
driver = "mysql"

[smarty]
smarty.caching = true
smarty.cache_lifetime = 3600
smarty.auto_escape = true
site.app_path = /src/jephy-mvc/app
site.home_root_path = src/public


[security]
smarty.csrf_protection = true
smarty.session_secure = true          # Force HTTPS sessions
smarty.session_httponly = true

[cache]
site.enabled = true
site.driver = "file"                # file, redis, memcached

Performance Optimization

1. Enable OpCache (PHP)


; In php.ini
opcache.enable=1
opcache.memory_consumption=128
opcache.max_accelerated_files=10000
opcache.revalidate_freq=0

2. Enable Smarty Caching


// In your controllers
$this->view->caching = true;
$this->view->cache_lifetime = 86400; // 24 hours

3. Enable Gzip Compression

Apache (.htaccess):

apache


    AddOutputFilterByType DEFLATE text/html text/css text/javascript application/javascript
Nginx:

nginx

gzip on;
gzip_types text/html text/css application/javascript;
4. Database Optimization:

sql

-- Add indexes to frequently queried columns
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);

-- Use query caching (MySQL)
SET GLOBAL query_cache_size = 67108864;
5. CDN for Static Assets

; config.conf
[paths]
assets_url = "https://cdn.yourdomain.com/assets/";

Security Hardening

For All Environments

# 1. Restrict config.conf access
chmod 600 jephy-mvc/config.conf

# 2. Disable directory listing
# Add to public/.htaccess
Options -Indexes

# 3. Protect sensitive directories
# Add to jephy-mvc/.htaccess
Deny from all

Cloud-Specific Security

AWS:

  • Use Security Groups (only allow 80, 443)
  • Enable AWS WAF
  • Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager

GCP:

  • Use Cloud IAM roles
  • Enable VPC firewall rules
  • Use Cloud KMS for encryption

Azure:

  • Use Network Security Groups
  • Enable Azure WAF
  • Use Key Vault for secrets

Monitoring & Maintenance

Logging

php

// Jephy-MVC logging
\Core\Logger::info('User logged in', ['user_id' => $userId]);
\Core\Logger::error('Database connection failed');

Health Check Endpoint

Create public/health.php:

<?php
// Simple health check for load balancers
$status = ['status' => 'healthy', 'timestamp' => time()];
header('Content-Type: application/json');
echo json_encode($status);

Automated Backups


# Cron job for database backup (VPS)
0 2 * * * mysqldump -u user -p'password' dbname > /backups/db-$(date +\%Y\%m\%d).sql

# AWS RDS automated backups (enable in console)
# GCP Cloud SQL automated backups
# Azure Database automated backups

Troubleshooting Deployment

Platform Common Issue Solution
Shared Hosting Path errors Use absolute paths in config.conf
VPS Permission denied chown -R www-data:www-data jephy-mvc/app/cache/
AWS 403 errors Check security group inbound rules
GCP Session issues Configure Cloud Datastore for sessions
Azure Slow performance Scale up App Service plan tier

Deployment Checklist

  • debug = false in config.conf
  • Smarty caching enabled
  • Database credentials updated
  • File permissions set (cache directory writable)
  • SSL certificate installed
  • .htaccess or Nginx config verified
  • Base URL configured correctly
  • Error logging enabled
  • Backup strategy in place
  • Monitoring setup complete

Quick Reference: Platform Commands

Platform Deploy Command Restart Command
Shared Hosting FTP Upload Contact host
VPS (Nginx) git pull sudo systemctl restart nginx php8.1-fpm
AWS EB eb deploy eb restart
GCP App Engine gcloud app deploy Auto-managed
Azure App Service git push azure main az webapp restart

ON THIS PAGE
